_comment: Wizard step definitions for each AI tag. Loaded by the CSR wizard widget.
_source: Sadie Hardy — New Wizard Step Flow doc (Mar 2026)

needs_tag.label: Needs Tag
needs_tag.intro: The AI was unable to classify this ticket. Human review is required.

steps.text: Read the full email carefully and determine the customer's actual need
Search parkm.app by customer email for context
Classify manually: What does this customer actually need?
steps.substep: Set the 'Agent Corrected Tags' field to the correct classification so the AI can learn from this case.

steps.text: Follow the appropriate process for the correct tag type

validation_on_close: Did you set the Agent Corrected Tags field?
Did you handle the underlying request?

customer_canceling_a_permit_and_refundin.label: Cancel Permit + Refund
customer_canceling_a_permit_and_refundin.intro: Customer wants to cancel their permit and/or get a refund.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle/permit in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'

steps.text: Confirm if the permit is active or cancelled

branches.label: Active
branches.description: Set it to delay cancel in 1 week.
suggestions: Actions > Cancel > Delay Cancellation and set it to 1 week out.
Actions > Edit > Delete the 'Next Recurring Date' so they are not charged again.

branches.label: Cancelled
suggestions: Check the 'Payment/Transaction' tab
Check the 'Stripe' tab for any active subscriptions that need to be cancelled.
Email Internalmanagers@parkm.com if the Stripe subscription needs to be cancelled.

steps.text: Does the resident qualify for a refund?
suggestions: Open 'Payments/Transactions' tab to locate the charge
If the charge was made within the last 30 days, send a refund request to accounting.
If the charge was made over 30 days ago, they do not qualify for a refund. Send the terms and conditions link to the resident.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit
Screenshot of account preview
Eligibility of refund (approved or denied — send terms and conditions link if needed)
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: Waiting on accounting (if a refund request was sent to accounting).
Waiting on Customer Response (if additional information is needed).
Closed (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you verify refund eligibility (30-day window)?
Did you set the permit to delay cancel (not immediate)?
Did you forward to accounting@parkm.com (if eligible) OR send denial?

customer_inquiring_for_grandfathered_per.label: Grandfathered Permit Inquiry
customer_inquiring_for_grandfathered_per.intro: Customer is inquiring about a grandfathered permit.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle/permit in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'

steps.text: Do they currently have a grandfathered permit on the vehicle in question?

branches.label: Yes
branches.description: Send the resident confirmation of their grandfathered permit and highlight the expiration date/time.

branches.label: No
branches.description: Check if they had one in the past and let the resident know when it expired.

steps.text: Does the resident need a new grandfathered permit?

branches.label: Yes
branches.description: Must have approval from their leasing office. Direct them to the leasing office or ask the resident to CC the leasing staff into the email thread for approval. The leasing office/property manager MUST email support@parkm.com to approve a grandfathered permit. The resident cannot self-approve.

branches.label: No
branches.description: Send the resident confirmation of their grandfathered permit and highlight the expiration date/time.

steps.text: Waiting on confirmation from the leasing office?
suggestions: Ensure the resident's vehicle is permitted while we wait for confirmation
Issue a free temporary permit for 1 week if needed

steps.text: Once ParkM Support has complex approval to issue the grandfathered permit
suggestions: Locate the vehicle/permit in question
Issue free permit (labeling it 'grandfathered' if needed)
Set the permit to expire in 1 year if an end date is not given by the complex

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit (temporary permit or grandfathered permit + expiration date)
Screenshot of account preview
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: Waiting on Customer Response (if additional information/confirmation is needed from the resident or the complex).
Closed (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you verify if the resident has or needs a grandfathered permit?
Did you respond to the resident with the permit status?

customer_inquiring_for_locked_down_permi.label: Locked Down Permit Inquiry
customer_inquiring_for_locked_down_permi.intro: Customer is inquiring about a locked down (sold out) permit.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Confirm the permit(s) show as 'Sold Out' for the resident
suggestions: Log into the resident's account
Go through the checkout process as if you were trying to purchase the permit in question (do not actually checkout)

steps.text: Sold out?

branches.label: Yes
branches.description: Send the thread to Internalmanagers@parkm.com and let the resident know you are looking into this and will respond shortly.

branches.label: No
branches.description: Respond to the resident with screenshots (from the resident side) of how they can locate and purchase the permit in question.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit
Screenshot of account preview or any other relevant screenshots
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you verify the permit is sold out for the resident?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_inquiring_for_additional_permit.label: Additional Permit Inquiry
customer_inquiring_for_additional_permit.intro: Customer is inquiring about getting an additional permit.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Check how many permits the resident currently has
suggestions: Look at the 'Vehicles and Permits' tab. Note how many active permits exist. Note the permit type(s) as well.

steps.text: Confirm the property's permit details
suggestions: Locate the property in .APP to confirm the current permit types offered
Go to Zoho CRM and view the property notes
Some properties cap the number of permits per leaseholder, per unit or per permit type.

steps.text: Is the resident allowed an additional permit?

branches.label: No
branches.description: Inform the resident of the limits set by their property and that the leasing office must email support@parkm.com to approve an additional permit. The resident can also CC their leasing office into the email thread.

branches.label: Yes
branches.description: Log into the resident account to confirm if they already have access to one.
suggestions: If they do, respond to the resident with screenshots/instructions on how to obtain this additional permit.

If they do not, apply an override to the resident's account (only once approved by property. Anyone that needs a permit override MUST have permission from the community leasing office. We need a direct email from the leasing office confirming that the person is allowed an extra permit.)

steps.text: Waiting on confirmation from the leasing office?
suggestions: Ensure the resident's vehicle is permitted while we wait for confirmation
Issue a free temporary permit for 1 week if needed

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit/override
Screenshot of account preview or any other relevant screenshots
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you verify the property's permit limits?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_rental_car.label: Rental Car
customer_rental_car.intro: Customer needs a temporary permit for a rental vehicle.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Confirm the customer has an existing active permit on their regular vehicle
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'
Their regular permit should stay as is. We do NOT want to modify it because they often forget to switch back.

steps.text: Request the rental vehicle information (if not already given) and how long they expect to have the rental
suggestions: If they say 2 weeks, issue for 4 weeks to allow buffer time. Never set the end date to 2055.
Issue for 4 weeks if they do not give an end date.

steps.text: Issue a FREE temporary permit for the rental vehicle
suggestions: Actions > Sale Permit > Select community > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration (double the expected rental time). Label it 'Temp permit' under custom permit number. Add rental vehicle info (plate, make, model, etc.). Do NOT enter a Next Recurring Date.
A free temporary permit can be issued as soon as you have the vehicle's license plate number. All other vehicle information can be requested after issuing the permit.

steps.text: Send confirmation to customer
suggestions: Screenshot of permit/highlight expiration date
Any additional questions or vehicle information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'Closed' (The resident has an active temp permit for rental vehicle(s) and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you issue a FREE temp permit for the rental vehicle?
Did you leave the regular permit unchanged?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_double_charged_or_extra_charges.label: Double Charged / Extra Charges
customer_double_charged_or_extra_charges.intro: Customer is reporting a double charge or unexpected extra charges.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle/permit in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'
Confirm the said vehicle is properly permitted and properly being charged
Are there other vehicles/permits being charged in the account?

steps.text: View the 'Payments & Transactions' and 'Stripe' tabs
suggestions: Look for duplicate charges, duplicate subscriptions, failed payment notices, convenience fees, or credit card surcharges.

steps.text: Determine the cause of the charge
common_causes: Convenience fee / credit card surcharge (these are normal and won't be the same price of the permit).
Resident had 2 permits for one vehicle
Resident had an additional vehicle permitted that they no longer need permitted
Another resident has their card on file (often times family/friends/roommates)
Actual duplicate charge/duplicate stripe subscription (system error)

NSF fee (NSF fees are when a customer has a bank account on file, and the payment fails/bounces. A $4 NSF fee is associated with this. The next time the resident tries to pay for a permit, the total payment amount due will be the price of the permit plus the NSF fee.)

steps.text: Legitimate charge (not duplicate)
suggestions: Explain to resident

steps.text: Double/duplicate charge found
suggestions: Cancel duplicate permit if appropriate
Forward to accounting for refund (only if the charge was made in the last 30 days)
Forward to internalmanagers@parkm.com if a duplicate stripe subscription needs to be cancelled

steps.text: Double/duplicate charge NOT found
suggestions: Request a screenshot of the double charges from the resident's bank statement (showing the amount, date of charges) and the last 4 digits of the card charged.
Forward to internal for assistance locating the duplicate charge once the resident has provided the necessary information.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of charge
Screenshot of account preview and/or any other relevant screenshots
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you determine the cause of the charge?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_guest_permit_and_pricing_questi.label: Guest Permit & Pricing
customer_guest_permit_and_pricing_questi.intro: Customer has questions about guest permits or pricing.

steps.text: Check the property's guest permit setup in .APP
suggestions: Go to the property > permit types > guest permits

steps.text: Check the property notes on guest permits in Zoho CRM
Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: The answer(s) to the resident's question(s) about guest permits: Pricing, Pre-purchasing, Extending, Property limits on guest permits
Screenshot of guest preview (Go to property > permit types > preview > guest/visitor)
Any other relevant screenshots
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you check property guest permit setup?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_miscellaneous_questions.label: Miscellaneous Question

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Identify the questions and search for the answers
suggestions: Check the property's notes in .APP
Check the property notes in Zoho CRM

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: The answer(s) to the resident's question(s)
Screenshot of account preview and/or any other relevant details
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you answer the resident's question?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_sending_money_order.label: Customer Money Order
customer_sending_money_order.intro: Customer wants to pay by money order.

steps.text: Confirm the resident has a ParkM account
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Confirm the Vehicle information and how long they are permitting the permit for
suggestions: Must be at least 6 months

steps.text: Issue a Money Order permit from the backend
suggestions: Actions > Sale Permit > Select the 'Check/Money Order' option > Confirm the Community is correct and that they are set up as a resident and not Guest/Visitor > Select the correct permit type > Edit the Expiration Date (and the time) and double check that the permit price is correct > Select (or add) the correct vehicle > TRIPLE check that all of the information is correct > Toggle 'ON' 'do not send an email receipt to customer' and title it 'Money Order' > Press SAVE > Go back to the permit on the admin side and press EDIT > Delete the 'Next Recurring' Date > Go to the resident's 'account preview' and double check that everything is correct.
There should be an expiration date (not a renewal date) and it should say 'Money Order'.

steps.text: Send the resident the 'Money Order' instructions
suggestions: Payment Requirements: If you want to pay by money order, you are required to purchase permits for a minimum of 6 months and a maximum of one year.
Payment Instructions: Send a money order payable to ParkM to: ParkM, F2-247, 4950 S. Yosemite St, Greenwood Village, CO 80111. Do not submit payments to your leasing office.
Information Needed: Include with your money order a slip of paper stating your ParkM account name, complex name, and the license plate number(s) for which the payment applies.
Cancellation Policy: Failure to submit the required payment may result in cancellation of your permit(s).
Notification Requirement: Before sending a money order, email support@parkm.com to confirm the permit pricing and notify us of your intent to pay by money order.

steps.text: Inform internalmanagers@parkm.com that a Money Order is coming
suggestions: Who it is for
How much it is for
How long it is for

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Screenshot of account preview showing their 'Money Order' permit, highlighting the expiration date.

Inform the resident that they will get expiration notices in their ParkM account and in their email when their permits are close to expiring. If they wish to renew their permits by money order again, they will have to email support@parkm.com.
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you issue the Money Order permit correctly?
Did you inform internalmanagers@parkm.com?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_need_help_buying_a_permit.label: Help Buying a Permit
customer_need_help_buying_a_permit.intro: Customer needs help purchasing a permit.

steps.text: Confirm the resident has a ParkM account
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: If no account: Direct them to create one
suggestions: https://parkm.app/permit/community?forceChange=true

steps.text: Identify the issue preventing purchase
common_causes: Resident type is wrong (needs to be changed)

steps.text: Log in as the resident
suggestions: Identify the error/issue

steps.text: Resolve the resident's issue
suggestions: Send instructions/screenshots

Purchase the permit on the backend/or login as the resident to purchase the permit IF PERMISSION IS GIVEN. Actions > Sale Permit. Make sure they have a payment method on file and you have permission to charge it. Toggle OFF 'Issue Free Permit' and select their payment method.

steps.text: Can't resolve the system error/issue
suggestions: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with details of the error and issue a free permit for 1 week while Internal Managers investigate the error.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit/account
Screenshot of account preview and/or any other relevant screenshots
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Instructions/screenshots
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you identify and resolve the purchase issue?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_need_help_renewing_permit.label: Help Renewing Permit
customer_need_help_renewing_permit.intro: Customer needs help renewing their permit.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle/permit in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'

steps.text: Confirm if the permit is active or cancelled

branches.label: Active
branches.description: If it is still active — determine why they are inquiring about renewing.
suggestions: Is it set to expire soon?
Is there a renewal date set?
Are they receiving CC decline notices?
What other notices are they receiving?

branches.label: Cancelled
branches.description: Identify the reason it was cancelled.
suggestions: CC declines?
Cancelled by resident?
Cancelled by support?

steps.text: Resolve the resident's issue/ensure they are able to renew their permit
suggestions: Reactivate the permit if cancelled by accident less than a week ago (you can only reactivate the permit if it was 'canceled.' You will not see the reactivate button on 'expired' permits. Do not reactivate permits that were canceled due to credit card decline.) Actions > Reactivate > {free/one time paid permit: choose expiration date} {paid/recurring permit: choose next recurring date} > Save > Yes

Instruct the resident to pay the 'Due Balance' sticker or offer to do it for them if they are receiving CC decline notices and their permit is still active. (Send screenshots/instructions OR log in as the resident if you have their permission to 'run' their card).
Instruct the resident to purchase a new permit/double check their payment method is correct and has sufficient funds if their permit has expired due to CC declines.
Instruct the resident to purchase a new permit if their permit was cancelled more than 7 days ago.

steps.text: Can't resolve their issue?
suggestions: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with details of the error and issue a free permit for 1 week while Internal Managers investigate the error.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit/account
Screenshot of account preview and/or any other relevant screenshots
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Instructions/screenshots
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you determine why they can't renew?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_need_help_creating_an_account.label: Help Creating Account
customer_need_help_creating_an_account.intro: Customer needs help creating a ParkM account.

steps.text: Check if an account already exists
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.
The customer may already have an account and not realize it.

steps.text: If account exists
suggestions: Confirm it is their account and that the email address on file is correct

Once they confirm — Send a password reset: Click 'Reset Password' button on their account if the email address on file is correct OR manually reset it by clicking 'Manage User' > Copy username > Change password to 'Parking' > send the new login details:
Username: *Paste username*  |  Password: Parking  |  https://parkm.app/permit/community

steps.text: If account does not exist
suggestions: Send them instructions/screenshots on how to create one

steps.text: Error/issue causing them to not be able to create an account?
suggestions: Identify the error/issue (ask for screenshots).
Attempt to create an account as the resident to try and replicate the error/troubleshoot/solve it
Forward to Internalmanagers@parkm.com if you are unable to resolve the error and issue a free temp permit for 1 week if able

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit/account set up
Screenshot of account preview (if applicable) and/or any other relevant screenshots
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Instructions/screenshots of how to create an account or resolve the error/issue
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you help the resident with account creation?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_new_towing_legislation_no_parki.label: Towing Legislation / No Parking
customer_new_towing_legislation_no_parki.intro: Customer has questions about towing legislation or parking availability.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle/permit in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'

steps.text: Confirm if they are paying for an active permit
suggestions: Note the community name and permit type

steps.text: Remind the customer that ParkM sells permits, not parking spaces
suggestions: ParkM does not guarantee a specific parking spot (unless they have an assigned space). ParkM sells permits and does NOT tow, boot, or ticket vehicles.

steps.text: Direct them to their leasing office for parking availability concerns (if applicable)
suggestions: The leasing office manages the property and parking rules. They can address concerns about parking availability and enforcement with their towing company.

steps.text: If unsure how to respond: Escalate to Internal Managers
suggestions: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with the thread and summary if the customer is persistent or the situation is unclear.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Answer the resident's question(s)
Status of permit
Screenshot of account preview (if applicable) and/or any other relevant screenshots
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you explain that ParkM sells permits, not parking spaces?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_no_plate_or_expired_tags.label: No Plate / Expired Tags
customer_no_plate_or_expired_tags.intro: Customer has no license plate or expired tags on their vehicle.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Explain that ParkM permits are tied to license plates
suggestions: The license plate must always be accurate on their permit to avoid towing/booting/ticketing issues.

steps.text: Direct the customer to their leasing office
suggestions: They need to speak to their complex about the expired tags / no plates situation. In the meantime, they need to park off site since ParkM permits require valid plates.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: ParkM permits are tied to license plates and can't have expired tags
Status of permit
Screenshot of account preview (if applicable)
Screenshot of the 'Attention' notice the resident gets when adding a vehicle
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Any other relevant screenshots
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you explain the license plate requirement?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_someone_is_parking_in_my_spot.label: Someone in My Spot
customer_someone_is_parking_in_my_spot.intro: Customer is reporting that someone is parking in their assigned spot.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle/permit in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'
Confirm the vehicle is properly permitted with the correct space number

steps.text: Direct the customer to their leasing office
suggestions: ParkM does not enforce parking spaces or tow vehicles. The customer should contact their leasing office about the unauthorized vehicle. The leasing office can then contact the towing company if needed.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit
Screenshot of account preview (highlighting the space number)
Screenshot of the 'Attention' notice the resident gets when adding a vehicle
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Any other relevant screenshots

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident).
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you direct the customer to their leasing office?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_parking_space_not_in_dropdown.label: Space Not in Dropdown
customer_parking_space_not_in_dropdown.intro: Customer can't find their parking space number in the dropdown.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Confirm the space number is unavailable for the resident
suggestions: Log into the resident's account
Go through the checkout process as if you were trying to purchase the permit in question (do not actually checkout)
Note if the designated space number is available or not

steps.text: Space number status

branches.label: Available
branches.description: Send the resident step by step instructions with screenshots on how to purchase their permit and select the space number.
suggestions: If they are still unable to locate the space number after you confirm it is available, ask for screenshots of their view and/or any errors they are receiving. If they give you permission to charge their card on file, you can purchase the permit for them.

branches.label: Not Available
suggestions: Check the property's space list in .APP (Go to properties > search for the property > click spaces > search for the space number)
No space number? It might need to be added. (Email Internalmanagers@parkm.com)

If a space number does appear and there is a permit number next to it, click on the permit number to see who currently has it. (If a different resident obtains it, email the property without the resident on, and ask who should be in which space. Issue a free temp permit while you await the response.) Once the property confirms who should be in which space, make the necessary adjustments.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of permit and space #
Screenshot of account preview
Screenshot of free temp permit (if applicable) and informing them you have reached out to their leasing office
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Any other relevant instructions/screenshots
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you verify the space availability?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_password_reset.label: Password Reset
customer_password_reset.intro: Customer needs a password reset for their ParkM account.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Verify the account exists and the email matches
suggestions: Does the email address on the ParkM account match the email address they are using to email support?
Does the resident have multiple ParkM accounts? Forward to Internalmanagers@parkm.com to merge accounts if needed.
Confirm the correct email address if necessary.

steps.text: Reset password using one of these methods
suggestions: Option 1: Direct them to https://parkm.app/account/login > 'Forgot Password'
Option 2: Find their correct account > click 'Reset Password' button
Option 3: Manual reset via Manage User > change password to 'Parking' > Send login credentials: Username: [their email] | Password: Parking | https://parkm.app/permit/community

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of account/password reset
Screenshot of account preview
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Any other relevant instructions/screenshots
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you reset the password or provide instructions?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_towed_booted_ticketed.label: Towed / Booted / Ticketed
customer_towed_booted_ticketed.intro: Customer's vehicle was towed, booted, or ticketed.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Verify the vehicle license plate number
suggestions: Is the resident's vehicle in question properly permitted?
Does the license plate number provided by the resident match the plate number on the permit?
Note any updates made to the vehicle recently
Note the vehicle details (VIN, year, make, model, color)
Review the other vehicles/permits in the account (are there duplicates?)

steps.text: Understand what happened
suggestions: Review the 'Tow Incidents' section in the resident's account. Click on it > View the 'Incident Detail' report showing pictures/details of the tow.

Review past email communication with the resident. Read the previous thread history and search in Zoho Desk for other email threads with information. (Try searching by resident name, email address and license plate number.)
Review the 'Communication' section in the resident's account (were they sent notices?)
Review the 'Notes & Activity' section
Were they previously warned? (If so, why?)
Check if the property they were towed from matches the property their permit is for
Is ParkM at fault?

steps.text: Determine if a proof of permit is applicable
suggestions: Only provide proof of permit if the vehicle appeared to have a permit at/around the time of the incident. Proof of permits cover the last 72 hours (3 days) only. If the incident was more than 72 hours ago, they need to work with the tow company using their original permit receipt.

steps.text: Remember that ParkM is NOT the tow company
suggestions: ParkM simply sells parking permits. We do not tow, boot, or ticket vehicles. Express empathy: 'I understand how frustrating being towed/booted can be...'

steps.text: If permitted: Send Proof of Permit
suggestions: Go to account > Actions > Proof of Permit. Either email it directly (add tow company email if known) or download the PDF and attach it to the thread. Check Zoho CRM for tow company email. After sending, STAY OUT OF IT — do not promise refunds or releases!

steps.text: If not permitted: Direct to tow company
suggestions: Look up the tow company for the property in Zoho CRM or in .APP under the property's enforcement company. Share the tow company PHONE NUMBER (not email). If the tow company is unknown, advise them to look for towing signs posted at the property or ask their leasing office for it.

steps.text: NEVER promise or mention a refund or release of the vehicle
suggestions: Tow/boot refunds are EXTREMELY rare and require Internal Manager approval. If the resident continues to email and persist, forward to internalmanagers@parkm.com for any assistance.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of vehicle/permit in question
Screenshot of account preview
Proof of permit (if applicable)
Tow company info (if applicable)
Remind the resident that ParkM is NOT the tow company
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
Any other relevant screenshots (sent notices, etc)
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you verify the vehicle's permit status?
Did you send proof of permit (if applicable)?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_warned_or_tagged.label: Warning / Tag
customer_warned_or_tagged.intro: Customer received a warning sticker or tag on their vehicle.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'
Confirm if the vehicle is properly permitted (is the license plate correct?)

steps.text: Locate the warning notice (this helps identify the reason for the tag and who placed it)
suggestions: Review the incident detail report in the 'History' or 'Tow Incidents' sections
ParkM Monitoring tags are Orange. Other colors = not us
Ask the resident to send a picture of the warning notice if necessary

steps.text: Why were they warned?
common_causes: Parked in handicap spot without placard

steps.text: Resolve the issue
suggestions: If not permitted: help them get a permit
If plate was wrong: update the plate
If other violation: explain and advise

If incorrectly warned: email Brian@parkm.com, mscherer@parkm.com and Internalmanagers@parkm.com letting them know. (You should always assume that the monitoring is correct. Before jumping the gun, make sure you look into WHY they were tagged and WHAT PLATE was tagged vs. what shows on ParkM as permitted or not.)
*Note: sticker removal is the customer's responsibility — ParkM cannot remove it.

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of vehicle/permit in question
Screenshot of account preview/warning
Instructions/screenshots on how to purchase a permit if needed
Updated plate if applicable
Explanation of violation
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you identify why they were warned?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_payment_help.label: Payment Help
customer_payment_help.intro: Customer needs help with a payment issue.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Review the 'Stripe' & 'Payments & Transactions' tabs
suggestions: Check for failed payments, expired cards, or missing payment methods, duplicate/inactive stripe subscriptions

steps.text: Identify the payment issue
common_causes: Wrong payment method set as default

steps.text: Resolve the payment issue and respond to the resident
suggestions: Direct them to https://parkm.app/account/login to update their payment method.
Delete the old method on the backend for them: Payments tab > delete old card.
Issue a free temporary permit

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of vehicle/permit in question/screenshot of account preview
Instructions/screenshots on how to update their payment method/remove it
Explanation of fees
Screenshot of CC Decline notices/instructions on how to pay their due balance (if applicable)
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident
"We are looking into this and will respond shortly" (if Internal is involved)

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'On Hold' if you're waiting on a response from Internal Managers for assistance.
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you identify the payment issue?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_update_vehicle_info.label: Update Vehicle Info
customer_update_vehicle_info.intro: Customer needs to update their vehicle information.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Locate the vehicle in question
suggestions: Check the 'Vehicles/Permits' tab
Check the 'Relationships' section in 'Overview'
Confirm the vehicle is properly permitted

steps.text: Determine what needs to be updated
suggestions: License plate number: Actions > Modify License Plate > Enter new plate > Toggle 'Transfer All Active Permits' > Save
All other changes (make, model, VIN, color): Actions > Edit Vehicle > Make changes > Save

steps.text: Verify the change saved correctly
suggestions: Refresh the page and confirm the update is reflected correctly
Go to their 'Account Preview' and confirm the update is correct

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of vehicle/permit in question/screenshot of account preview
Instructions/screenshots on how to update their vehicle information themselves next time
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you update the vehicle information?
Did you respond to the resident?

customer_update_contact_info.label: Update Contact Info
customer_update_contact_info.intro: Customer needs to update their contact information.

steps.text: Locate resident account in .APP
suggestions: Search by email address, name, license plate number, unit number or space number.
Try filtering the search by community if needed.

steps.text: Identify what needs to be updated
suggestions: Common updates: email address, phone number, unit number, apartment complex

steps.text: Update the info
suggestions: Go to the resident's account > Edit to update > Primary email address > Mobile phone > Unit > Save (DO NOT use Manage user)

steps.text: Verify the change saved correctly
suggestions: Refresh the page and confirm the update is reflected correctly

steps.text: Respond to the resident with all necessary information
suggestions: Status of vehicle/permit in question/screenshot of account preview
Screenshot of updated information
Any additional questions or information needed from the resident

steps.text: Update ticket status
suggestions: 'Waiting on Customer Response' (if additional information is needed from the resident or the property).
'Closed' (All necessary parties have been contacted and nothing else needs to be done on our end).

validation_on_close: Did you update the contact information?
Did you respond to the resident?

property_changing_resident_type_for_appr.label: Property: Change Resident Type
property_changing_resident_type_for_appr.intro: Property manager is requesting a resident type change so a resident can purchase a specific permit type (e.g., carport, garage).

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Confirm which resident type the property is requesting
steps.substep: Check the property notes in Zoho CRM. Example: at some properties, residents need to be 'Resident X' to get carport/garage permits.

steps.text: Change the resident type
steps.substep: Go to the resident's account > Edit > Change 'Customer Classification' to the requested type > Save.

steps.text: Notify the property that the change has been made
steps.substep: Let them know the resident can now purchase the approved permit type.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you change the resident type as requested?
Did you notify the property?

property_approving_grandfathered_permit.label: Property: Approve Grandfathered Permit
property_approving_grandfathered_permit.intro: Property is approving a grandfathered (free) permit for a resident. Issue a free permit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name (from property's email)
Get the vehicle details from the property's email
steps.substep: Need: license plate, make, model, year, color, state. If missing, ask the property.

steps.text: Issue a free grandfathered permit
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Select community > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to the date provided by property (if none given, default to 1 year and adjust later). Label permit 'Grandfathered'. Do NOT enter a Next Recurring Date.

steps.text: Confirm with the property that the permit has been issued
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the free permit and label it 'Grandfathered'?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_approving_override_additional_p.label: Property: Approve Override/Additional Permit
property_approving_override_additional_p.intro: Property is approving an override so a resident can get an additional or restricted permit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Open up permits or change resident type as needed
steps.substep:
Depending on the property setup, you may need to:
- Change the resident's customer classification
- Do an override to allow additional permits
- Open up a specific permit type

steps.text: Help the resident purchase or issue the permit
steps.substep: Either guide the resident to purchase online, or purchase/issue on the backend via Actions > Sale Permit.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you complete the override/permit issuance?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_extending_expiration_date_on_a_.label: Property: Extend Permit Expiration
property_extending_expiration_date_on_a_.intro: Property is requesting an extension on a permit's expiration date.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Locate the permit to extend
Determine the permit type and use the correct method
steps.substep:
RECURRING PAID permits: The 'Extend Expiration Date' button does NOT work on these. Use 'Move Next Recurring Date' instead.
FREE or ONE-TIME permits: Use either 'Extend Expiration Date' or 'Delay Cancellation' - both work.

steps.text: Extend the permit
steps.substep: Actions > Extend Expiration Date (or Move Next Recurring Date for recurring). Set the new date as requested by the property.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you use the correct method based on permit type?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_audits_or_reports.label: Property: Audit / Report Request
property_audits_or_reports.intro: Property is requesting an audit, report, or parking statistics.

steps.text: Identify what type of report the property needs
steps.substep: Common requests: permit list, vehicle list, payment history, space assignments, monitoring activity.

steps.text: Check if the report can be generated from parkm.app
steps.substep: Some reports can be pulled from the admin side. Check the property's data in .app.

steps.text: If you cannot generate the report: Forward to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with the property's request details.

steps.text: Send the report or let the property know it's being prepared
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide the report or escalate to Internal Managers?

property_checking_if_a_vehicle_is_permit.label: Property: Check Vehicle Permit
property_checking_if_a_vehicle_is_permit.intro: Property is asking if a specific vehicle/plate is permitted. Provide COMPLETE information - not just 'yes' or 'no'.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the license plate provided
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Check permit status and gather full details
steps.substep:
DO NOT just say 'yes'. Include ALL of:
- The date the permit became active on that plate
- The specific community the permit is for
- The type of permit (carport, garage, 1st car, open lot, etc.)
- Current status (active, expired, canceled)

steps.text: Understand the context
steps.substep: Properties often ask because a vehicle was tagged, booted, towed, or ticketed. A simple 'yes' can cause confusion - they may think the tow company made a mistake when the resident actually just updated their plate recently.

steps.text: Send detailed response to the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you include the activation date, community, and permit type?
Did you NOT just say 'yes' or 'no'?

property_checking_who_has_a_space_number.label: Property: Who Has This Space?
property_checking_who_has_a_space_number.intro: Property is asking who is assigned to a specific parking space number.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the space number at the property
steps.substep: Go to the property > check space assignments. Extracted space: {{space_number}}

steps.text: Find who has a permit with that space number
Respond with the resident's name and vehicle info for that space
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide the space assignment info to the property?

property_checking_who_is_in_a_unit.label: Property: Who is in This Unit?
property_checking_who_is_in_a_unit.intro: Property is asking who is registered in a specific apartment unit.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by unit number at the property
steps.substep: Extracted unit: {{unit_number}}

steps.text: Find residents registered for that unit
Respond with the resident name(s) and vehicle info for the unit
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide the unit info to the property?

property_inquiring_about_a_tow_boot_tick.label: Property: Tow/Boot/Ticket Inquiry
property_inquiring_about_a_tow_boot_tick.intro: Property is asking about a tow, boot, or ticket at their community. ParkM does NOT tow/boot/ticket. Be helpful but stay within our means.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the vehicle/plate in question
steps.substep: Extracted plate: {{license_plate}}

steps.text: Check permit status and tow history
steps.substep: Click on the vehicle > scroll down to 'Tow History'. Also check the account 'Overview' tab for tow incidents.

steps.text: Provide the property with the vehicle's permit status
steps.substep: Include: permit active date, community, permit type, and any violations/tow history logged.

steps.text: If property wants a tow/boot refunded: Forward to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Tow/boot/ticket refunds are only handled by Internal Managers. Forward the thread to internalmanagers@parkm.com with a summary.

steps.text: Direct the property to their towing company for tow-specific questions
steps.substep: Check Zoho CRM or .app for the property's enforcement company. Share the tow company contact info with the property.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you provide permit status information?
If refund requested, did you forward to Internal Managers?

property_inquiring_about_a_warning_tag.label: Property: Warning/Tag Inquiry
property_inquiring_about_a_warning_tag.intro: Property is asking about a warning tag placed on a vehicle.

steps.text: Check if ParkM monitors this property
steps.substep: Check Zoho CRM for 'ParkM Monitoring' checkmark. In Colorado, ParkM monitoring uses ORANGE tags.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the vehicle/plate in question
steps.substep: Check violations/tow history on the vehicle.

steps.text: If ParkM monitors: Explain why the vehicle was tagged
steps.substep: Check the violation log. Common reasons: no permit, expired permit, fire lane, handicap without placard, wrong spot, expired vehicle tags.

steps.text: If ParkM does NOT monitor: Direct to their monitoring company
steps.substep: Let them know ParkM did not place the tag. They should contact their monitoring/enforcement company.

steps.text: If something seems incorrect about the tag: Report to Brian McDonough and Internal Managers
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you check if ParkM monitors the property?
Did you respond to the property?

property_monitor_request.label: Property: Monitor Request
property_monitor_request.intro: Property is requesting monitoring, tagging, or patrol of their lot. This could be a request to tag/warn a specific vehicle, start monitoring their lot, or report an issue with current monitoring.

steps.text: Determine the type of monitoring request
steps.substep:
Is this a request to:
- Tag/warn a specific vehicle
- Start monitoring their lot
- Report an issue with current monitoring

steps.text: If requesting a specific vehicle to be tagged/warned
steps.substep:
Check if ParkM monitors the property (Zoho CRM 'ParkM Monitoring' checkmark).
IF WE MONITOR: Tell property we'll let our monitors know, then forward thread to Brian McDonough.
IF WE DON'T MONITOR: Direct them to their monitoring/towing company.

steps.text: If requesting to set up monitoring: CC the sales rep
steps.substep: CC the property's ParkM sales rep and let them handle the monitoring setup discussion.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you check if ParkM monitors the property?
Did you forward to Brian if applicable?

property_leasing_staff_login.label: Property: Leasing Staff Login
property_leasing_staff_login.intro: Property manager or leasing office staff needs help with their login to the Property Manager Portal.

steps.text: Determine if this is a new setup or a password reset
If password reset: Find the user in Administration
steps.substep: Go to Administration > Users > Paste their email > Toggle 'Show Tenant Users' ON. Click Actions > Reset Password (to send reset email) or Edit (to manually set password to 'Parking').

steps.text: If new setup: Forward to Internal Managers
steps.substep: New Property Manager portal setups should be handled by Internal Managers. Forward the request to internalmanagers@parkm.com.

steps.text: Send login credentials if reset
steps.substep:
Format:
Username: [their email]
Password: Parking
Portal: [Property Manager Portal URL]

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you resolve the login issue or escalate for new setup?

property_miscellaneous_questions.label: Property: Misc Question
property_miscellaneous_questions.intro: General question from a property manager that doesn't fit other categories.

steps.text: Read the full email carefully
Check Zoho CRM for property notes and sales rep info
If unsure: Email Internal Managers for guidance
steps.substep: Email internalmanagers@parkm.com with the thread and a summary. They will reply with guidance.

steps.text: Compose a clear, helpful response
steps.substep: Be extra helpful with property managers. Give thorough, informative answers.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you fully answer the property's question?

property_sending_money_order.label: Property: Money Order
property_sending_money_order.intro: Property is sending a money order payment on behalf of a resident.

steps.text: Get the resident's account info and vehicle details
Issue a free permit for the paid duration
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to match the paid period.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the free permit for the paid duration?

property_update_or_register_employee_veh.label: Property: Employee Vehicles
property_update_or_register_employee_veh.intro: Property wants to add or update employee vehicles. Employee vehicles get free permits for life (set expiration to 2055).

steps.text: Get the employee vehicle details from the property
steps.substep: Need: license plate, make, model, year, color, state, and employee name/email.

steps.text: Search for the employee in parkm.app or create an account if needed
Issue a free permit for life
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration to year 2055. You can also add the vehicle to the property's 'Employee Car' list.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the free permit with 2055 expiration?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_update_resident_vehicle.label: Property: Update Resident Vehicle
property_update_resident_vehicle.intro: Property is requesting a vehicle update on behalf of a resident.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Locate the vehicle to update
Make the update
steps.substep:
LICENSE PLATE: Actions > Modify License Plate > Enter new plate > Transfer All Active Permits > Save
OTHER: Actions > Edit Vehicle > Update fields > Save

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify the update saved?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_update_resident_contact_informa.label: Property: Update Resident Contact Info
property_update_resident_contact_informa.intro: Property is requesting a contact info update for a resident (email, phone, unit).

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's current email or name
Update the contact information as requested
steps.substep: Go to the account > Manage User or Edit to update email, phone, or unit number.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you update the info and confirm with the property?

property_update_resident_password.label: Property: Reset Resident Password
property_update_resident_password.intro: Property is requesting a password reset on behalf of a resident.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email
Reset the password
steps.substep:
Option 1: Click 'Reset Password' button on the account
Option 2: Manage User > change password to 'Parking'
Option 3: Administration > Users > search email > Toggle 'Show Tenant Users' > Actions > Reset Password

steps.text: Reply to the property with the resident's login info
steps.substep:
Format:
Username: [resident email]
Password: Parking
https://parkm.app/permit/community

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you reset the password and provide login info?

property_register_resident_account_for_t.label: Property: Register Resident Account
property_register_resident_account_for_t.intro: Property wants us to create a ParkM account for a resident.

steps.text: Get the resident's info from the property
steps.substep: Need: name, email, phone, unit number, and vehicle details (plate, make, model, year, color, state).

steps.text: Check if an account already exists for this email
If no account: Create one in parkm.app
steps.substep: Create the account with the resident's info and add their vehicle.

steps.text: Confirm with the property and provide login credentials
steps.substep:
Format:
Username: [resident email]
Password: Parking
https://parkm.app/permit/community

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you create the account and provide login info?

property_cancel_resident_account.label: Property: Cancel Resident Account
property_cancel_resident_account.intro: Property is requesting cancellation of a resident's permit/account.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Locate the active permit(s)
Set the permit to Delay Cancel (1 week out)
steps.substep: Do NOT cancel immediately. Use Actions > Cancel > Delay Cancellation > set to 1 week. Delete the 'Next Recurring Date' so they are not charged again.

steps.text: If the property/resident also wants a refund: Follow refund process
steps.substep: Forward to accounting@parkm.com with the required info.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you delay cancel (not immediate cancel)?
Did you delete the Next Recurring Date?
Did you confirm with the property?

property_permitting_paid_resident_vehicl.label: Property: Permit Paid Vehicle for Resident
property_permitting_paid_resident_vehicl.intro: Property wants us to purchase a PAID permit on behalf of a resident. Must have a payment method on file and permission to charge.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email
Verify the resident has a payment method on file
steps.substep: Go to Payments & Transactions tab. If no payment method, the resident will need to add one at https://parkm.app/account/login first.

steps.text: Confirm you have permission to charge the payment method
steps.substep: The property or resident must explicitly give permission before you charge.

steps.text: Purchase the permit on the backend
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Select community and permit type > Toggle OFF 'Issue Free Permit' > Select vehicle > Select payment method (credit card) > Toggle OFF 'Do not send email receipt' (so resident gets receipt) > Save.

steps.text: Confirm with the property
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you verify payment method on file?
Did you have permission to charge?
Did you send the receipt to the resident?

property_resident_payment_help.label: Property: Resident Payment Help
property_resident_payment_help.intro: Property is helping a resident with a payment issue.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email
Check Payments & Transactions tab for issues
steps.substep: Look for: failed payments, expired cards, missing payment methods, duplicate charges.

steps.text: Identify and resolve the payment issue
steps.substep:
For card updates: Resident needs to update at https://parkm.app/account/login
For billing disputes: Check if charge is legitimate or needs refund
For failed payments: Check card expiry

steps.text: If refund needed: Forward to accounting@parkm.com
Respond to the property with resolution
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you resolve or escalate the payment issue?

property_guest_permits.label: Property: Guest Permits
property_guest_permits.intro: Property is asking about guest permit process, limits, or pricing.

steps.text: Check the property's guest permit setup in parkm.app
steps.substep: Go to the property > check guest permit types, pricing, duration limits, and restrictions.

steps.text: Answer the property's question
steps.substep:
Key info:
- Guest permits are purchased by the RESIDENT for their guest
- Some properties have limits on guest permits
- Guests can pre-purchase and extend permits
- Guest permits vs resident permits are different permit types

steps.text: If the property wants to change guest permit settings: CC the sales rep
steps.substep: CC the property's ParkM sales rep for configuration changes.

steps.text: Send response and close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you answer the property's guest permit question?

property_potential_leads.label: Property: Potential Lead
property_potential_leads.intro: A new property or complex is interested in ParkM services. Forward to the sales team.

steps.text: Gather the lead information
steps.substep: Note: property name, contact name, email, phone, and what they're interested in.

steps.text: Forward the thread to the sales team
steps.substep: Forward to the appropriate sales channel. Check Zoho CRM for the territory/region.

steps.text: Acknowledge the inquiry to the property
steps.substep: Let them know a sales representative will be in touch shortly.

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you forward to the sales team?
Did you acknowledge the inquiry?

sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_rel.label: Sales Rep: Vehicle Release Request
sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_rel.intro: Sales rep is asking for a vehicle to be released from a tow/boot. Tow/boot refunds and releases require Internal Manager approval.

steps.text: Search parkm.app for the vehicle in question
Check the vehicle's permit status at the time of the incident
Forward to Internal Managers for review
steps.substep: Let the sales rep know you'll send it to a manager to review. Forward the thread to internalmanagers@parkm.com with a summary.

steps.text: Set ticket to waiting and close when resolved

validation_on_close: Did you forward to Internal Managers?
Did you NOT promise a release?

sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_gra.label: Sales Rep: Grandfather Request
sales_rep_asking_for_a_vehicle_to_be_gra.intro: Sales rep is requesting a grandfathered (free) permit for a vehicle.

steps.text: Search parkm.app by the resident's email or name
Get vehicle details
steps.substep: Need: license plate, make, model, year, color, state.

steps.text: Issue a free grandfathered permit
steps.substep: Actions > Sale Permit > Toggle 'Issue Free Permit' ON > Set expiration (1 year default, or as specified). Label 'Grandfathered'. No Next Recurring Date.

steps.text: Confirm with the sales rep
Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you issue the grandfathered permit?
Did you confirm with the sales rep?

towing_or_monitoring_leads.label: Towing/Monitoring Lead
towing_or_monitoring_leads.intro: A towing, booting, ticketing, or monitoring company is reaching out. Forward to Brian McDonough (Enforcement Manager).

steps.text: Determine the nature of the inquiry
steps.substep: Is this a tow company wanting to get set up? A property asking about getting a tow company? Questions about ParkM's towing/monitoring integration?

steps.text: Forward to Brian McDonough
steps.substep: Brian is our Enforcement Manager. He handles setting up tow/boot/ticket/monitoring companies on ParkM. Forward the thread to Brian.

steps.text: If a property is asking about getting a tow company: CC the sales rep
steps.substep: CC the property's sales rep and say: 'I've cc'd your ParkM sales representative, [Name], to assist in selecting a towing, booting, or ticketing company for your community.'

steps.text: Close ticket

validation_on_close: Did you forward to Brian McDonough?

the_law_asking_for_information.label: Law Enforcement Request
the_law_asking_for_information.intro: Law enforcement is requesting information. Escalate to Internal Managers immediately.

steps.text: Do NOT respond directly to law enforcement
steps.substep: These requests must be handled by management.

steps.text: Forward immediately to Internal Managers
steps.substep: Forward the entire thread to internalmanagers@parkm.com with 'Law Enforcement Request' in the subject/summary.

steps.text: Set ticket status to Escalated/Waiting

validation_on_close: Did you forward to Internal Managers?
Did you NOT respond directly to law enforcement?
